questions.text: The general form of a linear equation in two variables is:
questions.explanation: Where a, b, and c are real numbers, and a and b are not both zero.

questions.text: A linear equation in two variables has:
questions.explanation: For every value of x, there is a corresponding value of y that satisfies the equation.

questions.text: The graph of a linear equation in two variables is a:
questions.explanation: All solutions of the equation ax + by + c = 0 lie on a single straight line.

questions.text: The equation x = 7, in two variables, can be written as:
questions.explanation: We can represent a single-variable equation by giving the other variable a zero coefficient.

questions.text: Any point on the line y = x is of the form:
questions.explanation: Since y = x, the x-coordinate and y-coordinate must be identical.

questions.text: The graph of y = 6 is a line:
options: Making an intercept 6 on both axes
questions.explanation: Equations of the form y = k are horizontal lines parallel to the x-axis.

questions.text: The graph of x = 3 is a line:
questions.explanation: Equations of the form x = k are vertical lines parallel to the y-axis.

The equation of x-axis is:
questions.explanation: Every point on the x-axis has a y-coordinate of zero.

questions.text: The equation of y-axis is:
questions.explanation: Every point on the y-axis has an x-coordinate of zero.

questions.text: Which of the following is a solution of the equation x - 2y = 4?

questions.explanation: If x=0, then 0-y=0 so y=0. (0,0) is a solution.

questions.text: Express y in terms of x for 2x - 3y = 12:
A linear equation in two variables can have at most how many points where it intersects the x-axis?
questions.explanation: A straight line (not parallel to x-axis) intersects it exactly once.

questions.text: Linear equation 2x + 3y = 9.35bar can be written in ax+by+c=0 form as:
questions.explanation: Move the constant to the left side.

questions.text: In equation 2x + 3y = 12, if x = 3, what is y?
The cost of a notebook is twice the cost of a pen. Write a linear equation to represent this.
questions.explanation: Let x be cost of notebook, y be cost of pen. Then x = 2y.

questions.text: If x = 2 and y = 1 is a solution of 3x + 4y = k, then k is:
Which of the following is not a linear equation in two variables?
questions.explanation: In a linear equation, the power of each variable must be exactly 1.

questions.text: The graph of the equation x + y = 4 intersects the y-axis at:
questions.explanation: At y-axis, x=0, so 0+y=4 => y=4.

questions.text: How many linear equations in x and y can be satisfied by x = 1 and y = 2?
questions.explanation: Infinite lines can pass through a single point (1, 2).

questions.text: If point (3, 4) lies on 3y = ax + 7, then value of a is:
Equation of a line parallel to y-axis and passing through (-3, -2) is:
questions.explanation: Parallel to y-axis means vertical line x = constant. It passes through x = -3.

questions.text: Equation of a line parallel to x-axis and passing through (1, 5) is:
questions.explanation: Parallel to x-axis means horizontal line y = constant. It passes through y = 5.

questions.text: The point of the form (a, -a) always lies on the line:
questions.explanation: Since x=a and y=-a, then x+y = a + (-a) = 0.

questions.text: x = 5, y = 2 is a solution of the equation:
Every solution of the linear equation is a ______ on its graph.
questions.explanation: The set of all solution points forms the line graph.

questions.text: Is x + y = 0 a linear equation in two variables?
questions.explanation: It fits the ax + by + c = 0 form where a=1, b=1, c=0.

questions.text: Which quadrant does not contain any solution of x + y = 10 (x,y > 0)?
questions.explanation: If both x and y are positive, the solutions are in Quadrant I. There are no solutions where both are negative.

questions.text: If x = y, then the graph makes an angle of ______ with the x-axis.
questions.explanation: The line bisects the angle between the axes.

questions.text: The graph of x = a is a line parallel to:
questions.explanation: x = constant is a vertical line.

questions.text: The graph of y = b is a line parallel to:
questions.explanation: y = constant is a horizontal line.

questions.text: Solution of 2x + 1 = x - 3 is:
A linear equation in two variables is satisfied by ______ points.
questions.explanation: There are infinite points on any straight line.

questions.text: If we multiply or divide both sides of a linear equation by a non-zero number, then the solution:
questions.explanation: Basic property of equality in equations.

questions.text: Positive solutions of ax + by + c = 0 always lie in the:
questions.explanation: Positive solutions imply both x and y are positive.

questions.text: Can a linear equation in two variables represent a vertical line?
questions.explanation: If b=0, we get ax + c = 0, which is x = -c/a (a vertical line).
